D1 Optics L1 Remember Atom

Why Sound Bends but Light Barely Does

compare wavelength to opening size and see why diffraction is dramatic for sound

A wave spreads through an opening by an angle set by the ratio of its wavelength to the opening width. Big ratio → strong bending.

Slide the wavelength-to-slit ratio λ/a. Watch the wavefronts fan out: near 1 the wave spreads almost hemispherically (like sound); near 0 it barely diverges (like light through a window).
